method: Cross-site recurrent administrative word-forms (L2/L3). Metric: n_shared = # word-forms (total count>=2) appearing in >=2 distinct sites. Null: site-label shuffle (permute site labels across inscriptions, preserving each site's inscription count and each inscription's word-form multiset), 2000 draws, one-sided p for 'more cross-site sharing than chance'. Positive control: Linear B (DĀMOS) seeded into 8 pseudo-sites via contiguous blocks of original sequence order (declared deviation: contiguous blocks, not round-robin, to preserve real within-site form clustering). False-positive control: 30 i.i.d. controls (tokens drawn i.i.d. by marginal form-frequency, partitioned into pseudo-sites of real site sizes).
result: MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE per the frozen mechanical rule: the PC DETECT criterion (LB must show n_shared significantly ABOVE the site-shuffle null, p<=0.05) was NOT met. Both LB and LA show n_shared significantly BELOW the site-label-shuffle null (p_greater=1.0 for both; p_less~0.0005 for both). The site-label-shuffle null structurally OVER-predicts cross-site sharing when one site dominates (Haghia Triada = 845/1341 inscriptions): shuffling scatters the dominant site's concentrated forms across smaller sites, inflating null n_shared above the observed value. The machinery itself is well-calibrated (i.i.d. false-positive rate = 0.0 across 30 controls) and detects a strong, consistent, opposite-direction signal in both corpora, but the frozen DETECT criterion assumed the wrong direction for this null, so the PC gate fails by construction and the verdict is mechanically MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE. NOTE: this is a null-structure/direction mismatch, not a calibration failure — the i.i.d. control confirms the test does not fire spuriously.
key_findings: FROZEN MECHANICAL VERDICT = MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE: the positive-control DETECT gate failed because LB (like LA) shows n_shared significantly BELOW the site-label-shuffle null (p_greater=1.0), not above it as the frozen DETECT criterion assumed. The verdict follows mechanically from the frozen rule; the operator does not adjudicate.

The site-label-shuffle null structurally OVER-predicts cross-site sharing under site-size dominance: Haghia Triada holds 845 of 1341 inscriptions (63%), so shuffling its concentrated word-forms across the many small sites inflates null n_shared (LA: null mean 202.8 vs observed 148; LB: null mean 1551.2 vs observed 1136). Both corpora are affected identically.

The machinery IS calibrated: across 30 i.i.d. false-positive controls (tokens drawn i.i.d. by marginal form-frequency, partitioned into pseudo-sites of real site sizes) the false-positive rate is exactly 0.0 — the test never fires spuriously when there is no identity<->site association. The failure is a null-structure/direction mismatch, not a calibration defect.

Supplementary direction diagnostic (NOT used for the frozen verdict): the 'less sharing than chance' one-sided p is ~0.0005 for BOTH LA and LB, i.e. word-forms in both corpora are significantly MORE site-localized than the site-label-shuffle null predicts. This is a strong, consistent, opposite-direction signal.

Leave-one-site-out (Haghia Triada removed): n_shared drops to 93, p_greater=1.0 (still below null). 62 word-forms still span >=3 sites among the non-HT sites, so a non-trivial shared core exists in raw counts — but it is still below what the shuffle null predicts.

The most cross-site word-forms are dominated by single-sign tokens (A spans 10 sites; TE 9; JA 9; VIN 8; KI 8) and high-frequency commodity/logogram signs (SI 7 sites/118 count; KU 5 sites/170 count; GRA 5 sites/62 count). These are ANONYMOUS sign sequences; no phonetic/meaning claim is made. A KU-RO-type total form is NOT among the top cross-site forms.
successor_hypotheses: H1 (NULL RE-SPECIFICATION): Re-formulate the null to test the ACTUAL operative direction — 'significantly LESS cross-site sharing than chance' (site-localization) — which both LA and LB satisfy at p~0.0005. This would re-enable the PC DETECT gate and allow a SHARED/SITE_LOCAL verdict on the site-localization hypothesis.

H2 (SITE-BALANCED NULL): Use a site-balanced resampling null that draws an EQUAL number of inscriptions per site (subsampling HT to match smaller sites) before shuffling, removing the dominance-driven inflation of null n_shared. Re-test whether specific forms then recur across sites more than this balanced null predicts.

H3 (FORM-FREQUENCY-STRATIFIED NULL): Stratify the shuffle by form-frequency class (rare vs common forms shuffled separately), since single-sign high-frequency tokens (KU, KA, SI) dominate the cross-site counts and may behave differently from multi-sign word-forms. Test whether MULTI-SIGN forms (length>=2) show cross-site sharing above a frequency-matched null.

H4 (EXCLUDE SINGLE-SIGN FORMS): Restrict the metric to multi-sign word-forms (length>=2), which are more likely to be genuine lexical/administrative items rather than commodity markers or logograms. Re-test cross-site recurrence on this restricted vocabulary.

H5 (PAIRWISE SITE-SITE SHARING): Instead of an aggregate n_shared, compute a pairwise site-site sharing matrix (Jaccard over word-form sets) and test whether specific site pairs (e.g. HT-Khania, Khania-Knossos) share more forms than a pairwise null predicts — this localizes where any shared register concentrates.

H6 (LB NATIVE PROVENANCE): If DĀMOS tablet provenance metadata can be recovered, rebuild the LB PC with REAL LB sites (Pylos, Knossos, Mycenae, Thebes) instead of a seeded pseudo-partition, giving a properly grounded positive control for cross-site administrative vocabulary.
non_circular: Word tokens carry NO phonetic/sound/meaning/reading. All statistics are pure L2/L3 distributional recurrence of ANONYMOUS sign sequences (tuples of signs). LB is used ONLY as a positive-control benchmark, never to read Linear A forms. The verdict is computed mechanically from the frozen rule in prereg.md; the operator never declares a scientific verdict. The supplementary 'less-than' p-value is reported for honesty about signal direction but is NOT used for the frozen verdict.
deviations: LB positive-control pseudo-partition uses SEEDED CONTIGUOUS BLOCKS of the original DĀMOS sequence order (chunked into tablet-sized pseudo-inscriptions), NOT round-robin. Declared in prereg.md before the LA verdict: round-robin artificially spreads every frequent form across all pseudo-sites and washes out the signal; contiguous blocks preserve real within-site form clustering. This is a PC partition-design choice only; it does not touch the LA metric, null, or verdict rule.

A supplementary 'less-than-chance' one-sided p-value (p_less) is reported in numbers for both LA and LB to honestly characterize the signal direction. It is NOT used for the frozen mechanical verdict (which tests only 'more sharing than chance').
